items.task: Get China SIM card or international plan
items.detail: Buy at airport, order online, or arrange through your carrier. A local SIM provides the best rates for data and calls within China.

items.task: Set up VPN for internet access
items.detail: Many international websites and apps are blocked in China. Install and configure a reliable VPN on all devices before arriving to ensure access to global internet.
Use apps like Baidu Maps or Google Maps (with VPN) to download offline maps of areas you'll visit. This helps with navigation without internet.
WeChat is essential for communication, payments, and accessing services in China. Create an account and add a payment method if needed.
Store key numbers: police (110), ambulance (120), fire (119), and your country's embassy. Add them to your phone contacts for quick access.
Bring a power bank to keep devices charged during long days out, as public charging stations may not be readily available.
China uses Type A, C, and I power outlets. Bring a universal adapter to charge your devices without issues.

items.task: Backup important data to the cloud
items.detail: Use cloud services to back up photos, documents, and contacts. This protects against data loss if your device is lost or stolen.
Install Alipay or link international cards to WeChat Pay for seamless cashless transactions. Many places only accept mobile payments.

items.task: Rent or buy a portable Wi-Fi hotspot
items.detail: Consider a portable Wi-Fi device for reliable internet on the go, especially if traveling with multiple devices or in groups.
